headroom.class_rule: saturated if |full-random| <= 2*std(random); controller-near-ceiling if (full-controller) <= pooled std; else open-headroom. Signs oriented so positive = improvement.

val_test_correlation.note: v5 rows mix configs within an arm (different noise fracs/channel drops), so cross-config spearman conflates config difficulty with val-test drift; leaderboard_val_test_spearman per seed (same pool, ~15-27 strategies) is the clean estimate.
val_test_correlation.v5_rows_direction_caveat: For timeseries rows new_val is a val_gain (NEGATIVE MASE, higher=better) while new_test is raw MASE (lower=better); verified on ts-ETTh1 rows (e.g. seed0 val=-1.00187, test=0.9871). Therefore the timeseries v5-rows spearman of -0.8394 (ETTh1-only -0.9429) indicates ALIGNMENT across configs, not drift. Cross-config spearman also conflates config difficulty. The clean per-arm drift estimate is pubcore_leaderboard (same pool, per seed, ~15-27 strategies).

old_claims.source: docs/code_signal_diagnosis.md (referenced by task as method_v2 text diagnostic)
old_claims.code_vs_dmf: DMF edges mmdataselect by ~1.2% on code (old stratified from-scratch regime, seed 0)
old_claims.general_vs_random: old table: mmdataselect 2472 vs random 2338 per-domain ppl (= +5.7% worse); a '~4.2%' figure is NOT-CAPTURED in any current doc

text.dmf_dsir_current: NOT-CAPTURED: no dmf/dmf_pub/dsir per-domain ppl rows exist in the current batches (text-controls-20260717T0026 has base/random/noselect; text-qz4-20260717T0933 has quadmix_pub/zip/mmds_adapt). The old 1.2% code-vs-DMF gap cannot be re-measured on current code state.

etth1_pick_evidence.source: outputs/timeseries/ETTh1/run_id=pubcore-paired-20260716T1754-*/seed_{0,1,2}/results.json adapt_manifest.chosen + results rows; seed2 canonical cell replaced per canonical_tables.final_cells.time_etth1.mmds_adapt.ts_seed2_replacement (quadmix style-proxy PROTOCOL_INVALID -> ctrl-ts2-noproxy re-run 1.0284)

per_seed.note: worst pick coincides with the worst leaderboard val-test rho (0.4545)

etth1_pick_evidence.reading: a different strategy is picked every seed; the pick quality tracks the per-seed leaderboard val-test rho; auth_only is stable (0.9501+-0.0053) and beats the controller mean by 0.031 MASE

tabular.evidence: full-random gain 0.0182 <= 2*std(random)=0.022 (saturated by rule); controller selected-set purity 0.5711 is BELOW random 0.6033 yet controller beats random on AUC (0.8734 vs 0.8671) and auth_only with purity 0.712 scores WORST-tier 0.8433 -> purity is uncorrelated with test AUC (TabPFN is robust to the injected noise, geometry/diversity dominates); ctrl-vs-kcenter -0.0019 within pooled std 0.0201; leaderboard val-test rho 0.947 (val probe is fine, candidates are near-tied).

tep.verdict: seed-noise at ceiling (controller-near-ceiling); NO actionable gap
tep.evidence: controller 0.4079 >= full 0.4057 (F1); gap to strongest fixed dmf_pub 0.409 is -0.0011 vs pooled std 0.0062; leaderboard val-test rho 0.886; purity 0.797 close to auth ceiling 0.838.

timeseries_ETTh1.verdict: real-mechanism-gap: validation-probe drift + raw argmax over near-tied candidates picks an unstable strategy; the ONLY actionable arm
timeseries_ETTh1.evidence: leaderboard val-test rho mean 0.6202 (per-seed 0.7212/0.6848/0.4545) vs 0.886-0.947 on the other three arms -> materially lower, drift hypothesis SUPPORTED; controller picks a different strategy each seed (fuse-variant/dmf_pub/quadmix) and seed2's bad pick lands 1.0284 vs auth_only 0.9461; controller 0.9811+-0.0421 vs auth_only 0.9501+-0.0053 (8x std, -0.031 MASE mean); formally still within pooled std 0.0424 but the pooled std is itself dominated by the controller's pick instability.

vision.verdict: undertraining-or-saturation (budget/quantity-dominated); NO actionable selection-quality gap despite 'open-headroom' label
vision.evidence: full-random 0.1575 (open headroom by rule) but full trains on 4000 vs budget 2000 -> quantity effect; controller purity 0.801 already at the auth_only ceiling (0.8013), residual flips 0.0075 (~eliminated); only 'flip' is a real perturbation in the vision recipe (dup/hard are tag-only markers, labels/features untouched), so no further purity headroom exists; Jaccard(ctrl, auth_only)=0.9191, Jaccard(ctrl, dmf_pub)=0.9286 -> selections already near-identical; ctrl-vs-auth_only -0.002 within pooled 0.0096.

text.verdict: no-signal-separation under the current finetune regime; NOT a candidate target
text.evidence: mmds_adapt vs random per-domain ppl deltas: code +0.024%, general +0.012%, image -0.194%, math -0.171%, table +1.706% -> old gaps (code ~1.2% vs DMF, general ~5.7%/claimed-4.2% vs random) do NOT persist; dmf/dsir rows NOT-CAPTURED in current batches.

mechanism_hypotheses_ranked.hypothesis: ETTh1 val-probe drift x near-tie argmax: the controller's validation leaderboard ranks strategies with rho ~0.45-0.72 against test on ETTh1 (vs ~0.89-0.95 elsewhere), and near-tied top candidates flip the argmax per seed; a tie-aware rule (deviate from a stable reference only beyond a paired-noise margin; else keep the reference) or a drift-robust val probe (blocked/rolling validation) targets the 0.031 MASE mean gap and the 8x std inflation vs auth_only.
mechanism_hypotheses_ranked.evidence: pubcore_leaderboard.timeseries per-seed rho; etth1_pick_evidence; final_cells time_etth1 mmds_adapt 0.9811+-0.0421 vs auth_only 0.9501+-0.0053

mechanism_hypotheses_ranked.hypothesis: Purity is saturated everywhere it matters: the controller already sits at the auth_only purity ceiling on vision (0.801 vs 0.8013) and near it on tep/ts; any candidate adding a better noise filter has NO remaining headroom; remaining full-vs-controller gaps (vision 0.115, tabular 0.012) are quantity/model-robustness effects, not selection quality.
mechanism_hypotheses_ranked.evidence: noise_purity_seed_mean all arms; vision recipe: only flip perturbs data; tabular purity-vs-AUC inversion (auth_only 0.712 purity -> 0.8433 AUC)

mechanism_hypotheses_ranked.hypothesis: The only cross-arm improvement space is pick-stability/variance reduction, not mean metric: on all four arms ctrl-vs-best is within pooled seed noise, and on 3 of 4 arms the controller pick differs per seed while test barely moves; a stability mechanism is defensible but metric-neutral except on ETTh1 where it also recovers mean.
mechanism_hypotheses_ranked.evidence: headroom.ctrl_vs_best_within_seed_noise true on all arms; docs/method_v2_diagnostic.md pick-instability table consistent with v5 rows
